data.reasoningText:
The user has migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

data.content:
The problem is clear. Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** ("no tests found") when all tests are compiled out via `#if INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS`. VSTest returned 0 in this case.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (enabled via `EnableMSTestRunner`), exit code 8 means "no tests were found." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, all tests are compiled out, and the test runner returns exit code 8.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` in the project file (or Directory.Build.props). This tells the testing platform to treat "zero tests discovered" as a success (exit code 0) instead of returning exit code 8.


Let me verify this works by building the project.

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.content:
**The fix:** Added `--ignore-exit-code 8` via `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>`.

Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag tells the runner to map that specific exit code back to 0, while still correctly reporting failures for actual test errors.
